text.title: Beta Charter Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Beta Charter Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Beta cohort MUST be ≤20 users for solo SaaS, ≤10 for founder-led B2B. Larger = the founder cannot respond personally and the beta degenerates into a free tier.
rule.rationale: Beta-program research — anchors Beta Charter Template rule `cohort-size-cap`.

rule.statement: Beta MUST have a fixed duration of 28-56 days, named at start. Open-ended beta = never graduates.
rule.rationale: Time-box discipline — anchors Beta Charter Template rule `duration-bounded`.

rule.statement: Charter MUST name exactly one success metric + threshold (e.g., 'weekly active use ≥3 sessions/user'). Multiple metrics = no signal.
rule.rationale: Goal-Question-Metric — anchors Beta Charter Template rule `single-success-metric`.

rule.statement: Founder MUST send a weekly broadcast to the cohort (status + ask + change). Silent weeks kill engagement.
rule.rationale: Beta-cohort engagement research — anchors Beta Charter Template rule `weekly-broadcast`.

rule.statement: Charter MUST list both 'graduate to paid' and 'kill the beta' criteria with thresholds. Without exit criteria the program runs forever.
rule.rationale: Falsifiability practice — anchors Beta Charter Template rule `explicit-exit-criteria`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
